skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit AI images through the inference.sh CLI using hosted models such as FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and Topaz.
skill.summary: Run hosted image generation, editing, stitching, and upscaling models from AI assistants through inference.sh.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution alerts are Markdown shell examples, not embedded Ruby or executable skill code. The skill intentionally allows the inference.sh CLI, which sends prompts and image URLs to hosted services, so it should publish with a network and privacy warning. No prompt injection attempt or weak cryptographic algorithm was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External Hosted Image CLI Sends User Content
medium_findings.description: The skill uses the inference.sh CLI to run hosted image models with user prompts and image URLs. This is legitimate for the skill, but users should avoid private or regulated content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly documents infsh commands and external image URLs. The behavior is expected, but third-party processing creates privacy and cost risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Example Inputs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, a hosted preview image, and placeholder image inputs. No evidence found that the skill silently contacts unrelated services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links or sample input values. They do not indicate hidden network behavior beyond the documented hosted service.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alert Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The static blocker points to the description line, but that line describes image models and use cases. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of line 3 shows descriptive metadata only. There is no cryptographic code or instruction at that location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick Command Alerts Are False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer interpreted Markdown backticks and fenced shell examples as executable code. They are documentation examples, not Ruby backtick execution inside a program.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All cited command patterns are inside Markdown inline code or fenced examples. No executable script file or dynamic shell interpolation is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Use Through infsh
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on infsh commands to access hosted image apps. This is intended functionality, but command use should stay limited to the documented CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are real CLI invocations users may run. They are not arbitrary code execution, but they do invoke an external tool.

dangerous_patterns.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
dangerous_patterns.description: Review found no text claiming system authority, asking the evaluator to ignore instructions, or claiming pre-approval. No evidence found of prompt injection in SKILL.md.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file contains model documentation, command examples, and links. It does not contain evaluator override language or system-message impersonation.

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with Hosted Models
content.value_statement: Designers and builders need quick access to many image models without changing tools. This skill turns image requests into inference.sh CLI runs for generation, editing, and upscaling.
actual_capabilities: Runs text-to-image prompts through documented inference.sh image apps.
Shows commands for FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and ImagineArt models.
Supports image editing workflows that use image URLs as inputs.
Provides examples for upscaling and stitching multiple images.
Lists related inference.sh image skills for focused workflows.
limitations: Requires a working inference.sh CLI installation and login.
Generated images depend on the selected hosted model and its availability.
Prompts and image URLs may be processed by external services.
The skill does not include local image generation code.

use_cases.title: Create Product Mockups
use_cases.description: Generate studio-style visuals for early product concepts before arranging a photoshoot.

use_cases.title: Explore Concept Art
use_cases.description: Test visual directions across several hosted models from one assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Prepare Social Visuals
use_cases.description: Create, edit, upscale, or stitch images for campaign drafts and content calendars.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [subject] in [style] with [lighting] using a fast model.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the same [scene] with FLUX, Gemini, and Seedream. Summarize which result best fits [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image URL: [url]. Change [specific area] to [desired result] while preserving [important details].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Production Visual Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [asset], upscale it, then stitch it with [second image URL] for a [format] deliverable.

output_examples.input: Create a product photo of a ceramic mug on a white background.
output_examples.output: A command-ready image generation request using a product photography prompt and a suitable hosted model.

output_examples.input: Upscale this draft image for a marketing page.
output_examples.output: An inference.sh upscaling workflow that uses the supplied image URL and returns a higher-resolution result.

output_examples.input: Make a poster that includes clear headline text.
output_examples.output: A text-rendering image request using a model documented for poster and typography tasks.

best_practices: Use specific prompts with subject, style, lighting, aspect ratio, and intended use.
Confirm model costs and output rights before using results in production.
Avoid sending private images or sensitive prompts to hosted models.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts when brand, layout, or text accuracy matters.
Do not assume every model supports editing, upscaling, or text rendering.
Do not submit confidential images unless your policy permits hosted processing.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh CLI to run hosted image models.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes. The quick start requires CLI login before running model commands.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents image editing and workflows that use image URLs.

faq.question: Can it upscale images?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Topaz upscaler example through inference.sh.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and image URLs may be sent to hosted services.
